clusters.title: Who is Iran's new ayatollah?

items.title: Who is Iran's new ayatollah?
items.standfirst: Mojtaba Khamenei has been appointed Iran’s new supreme leader, succeeding his father more than a week after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez reports on the new leader as he now takes over a country in turmoil.
items.content: Mojtaba Khamenei has been appointed Iran’s new supreme leader, succeeding his father more than a week after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez reports on the new leader as he now takes over a country in turmoil.

The son of the late leader has long been linked to Iran’s security establishment…

items.title: What to know about Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader
items.standfirst: The second son of the late supreme leader keeps a low profile. But he's long been viewed as wielding his power behind the scenes, from crushing dissent to influencing presidential elections.
items.content: The second son of the late supreme leader keeps a low profile. But he's long been viewed as wielding his power behind the scenes, from crushing dissent to influencing presidential elections.(Image credit: Atta Kenare)

items.title: What will Iran's new Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei be in charge of?
items.standfirst: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has become Iran's new supreme leader. The secretive 56-year-old cleric is only the third supreme leader in the history of the Islamic Republic and has close ties to the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard. But what will his role actually entail?

But what will his role actually entail?

clusters.title: Trump claims Iran or ‘somebody else’ could have carried out deadly school strike
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump Claims Iran May Have Carried Out School Strike

items.title: Trump claims Iran or ‘somebody else’ could have carried out deadly school strike
items.standfirst: President makes evidence-free claim despite video showing US Tomahawk missile hit naval base next to schoolAs oil prices surged amid the widening war with Iran, Donald Trump suggested, without evidence, on Monday that the strike on an Iranian elementary school could have been carried out by Iran or “somebody else”.During back-to-back appearances in Florida, Trump was asked whether the US would accept responsibility for a strike that hit the school and killed scores of people, many of them children, after video evidence showed a US Tomahawk struck the naval base next to it. Continue reading...
items.content: President makes evidence-free claim despite video showing US Tomahawk missile hit naval base next to schoolAs oil prices surged amid the widening war with Iran, Donald Trump suggested, without evidence, on Monday that the strike on an Iranian elementary school could have been carried out by Iran or “somebody else”.During back-to-back appearances in Florida, Trump was asked whether the US would accept responsibility for a strike that hit the school and killed scores of people, many of them children, after video evidence showed a US Tomahawk struck the naval base next to it. Continue reading...

items.title: Frustration mounts at airports amid TSA staffing shortages spurred by DHS shutdown
items.standfirst: Airline passengers in some of the United States' largest airports are seeing long security lines amid the ongoing partial government shutdown impacting the Department of Homeland Security. As Kris Van Cleave reports, more TSA employees are calling out sick as they work without pay.

clusters.title: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
clusters.neutral_headline: Istanbul Mayor On Trial For Corruption

items.title: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
items.standfirst: Ekrem Imamoglu is seen as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.
items.content: Ekrem Imamoglu is seen as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.

items.title: Jailed Istanbul mayor goes on trial in corruption case slammed as political
items.standfirst: Istanbul's former mayor Ekrem Imamoglu has gone on trial in Turkey on charges of corruption, in a landmark case widely criticised by rights groups. Imamoglu was arrested almost one year ago, when the opposition CHP party named him as their presidential candidate. Critics say the trial is an attempt to scupper Imamoglu's chances of beating Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the ballot box.
items.content: Istanbul's former mayor Ekrem Imamoglu has gone on trial in Turkey on charges of corruption, in a landmark case widely criticised by rights groups. Imamoglu was arrested almost one year ago, when the opposition CHP party named him as their presidential candidate. Critics say the trial is an attempt to scupper Imamoglu's chances of beating Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the ballot box.

ai_summary: Ekrem Imamoglu faces trial for corruption charges
Imamoglu seen as Turkish President Erdogan rival

Imamoglu arrested almost a year ago
Opposition party names Imamoglu as presidential candidate
